title: deyeuxia forsteri var. lyallii
intro: Deyeuxia forsteri var. lyallii, also known as Lyall's grass, is a type of grass native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Lyall's grass are said to include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to support immune function, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues.
